title: Create real-life workflows README
summary: Create a product-like README and npm-style installable skill package for real-life-workflows, including a generated hero, count proof, the first callable real-life-workflow-search skill, package validation, and quarantine-safe workflow packet behavior.
acceptance_criteria: README is operator-facing for developers who will use the project
README presents the project attractively and explains what it can do
README visibly advertises 171 curated workflow doors, 17 source repos, and a 19,000+ upstream-flow universe without implying every upstream flow is fully audited
README links to a project-local methodology or count artifact supporting the 19,000+ upstream-flow claim
README includes a compact breakdown of the 171 curated workflow doors instead of listing every door

README includes a clear features section covering workflow-understanding packets, deeper source research, source links, confidence/caveat handling, saved packet outputs, and future skill extraction paths
README positions the project as a reusable skill surface that developers can install npm-style, while avoiding API/import claims unless the implementation provides that surface
implementation creates the minimal npm-style package surface needed for README installation claims to be true
installed package exposes callable skill content for agent harnesses such as Claude Code, Codex, and opencode, not only packaged documentation
first callable packaged skill is named real-life-workflow-search
real-life-workflow-search writes packets to the caller or primary project's approved artifact area with provenance, never into node_modules or dependency control-plane files
README includes a polished visual hero asset generated with the GPT image generation model in Codex
README distinguishes active repo skills from quarantined imported source evidence
README points to the workflow-understanding finder and relevant project artifacts without duplicating tracker mechanics
README follows Matt Pocock style and ECC documentation placement rules
workflow state validation passes after the README task is implemented

context_snapshot.summary: Load real-life-workflows state, the quarantine boundary, the finder artifacts, testing-session summary, and the active pollution-audit tracker task before grilling or implementing the project README.

session_log.event: Initiated task after user requested a README for the real-life workflows project.
Moved task from intake to Matt-style grilling after loading root, registry, project state, non-done tasks, testing-session summary, quarantine manifest, finder docs, and Matt grilling references.
Grilling decision: README audience is operators, especially developers who may use the project; tone should be polished and promotional while explaining what the workflow finder can do.

Grilling decision: README should emphasize the larger catalog promise: 171 curated workflow doors across 17 source repos, backed by a 19,000+ upstream-flow source universe. Do not say 171 repos, and do not imply all 19,000+ upstream flows are fully audited local rows.

Grilling decision: the 19,000+ upstream-flow claim is valid for README positioning, but implementation should add or copy a project-local methodology/count artifact so operators can inspect the proof inside real-life-workflows.

Grilling decision: README should include a table showing all 171 curated workflow doors to make the catalog feel substantial, and should include a polished hero asset generated with the GPT image generation model in Codex.

Grilling correction: do not list all 171 doors in the README. Show a compact breakdown that communicates catalog scale, and add feature-focused sections for packet creation, deeper research, source/caveat handling, saved outputs, and future skill extraction.

Grilling decision: README primary feature story is searching a large workflow universe. Workflow-understanding packets should be presented as the practical output of that search, not the headline promise.
Grilling decision: README should feel product-like, positioning real-life-workflows as a polished workflow catalog for operators while staying grounded in the current local project surface.

Grilling decision: README should not frame this as a one-off script. It should present real-life-workflows as a reusable skill surface that developers can install, import, or invoke as skills; any npm/package wording must be backed by an actual install/import surface or clearly marked as intended packaging.

Grilling decision: the preferred install and usage surface is npm-style. README should prioritize npm-style installation/import/invocation language, with claims limited to the package surface actually implemented or explicitly marked as planned.

Grilling decision: implementation should create the minimal npm-style package surface needed to make README install, import, and invocation examples truthful instead of only documenting a future package idea.

Grilling correction: npm-style means the package should be installable first. Do not overbuild a public import/API surface unless it is needed to verify installability or the operator later asks for it.

Grilling decision: npm install should be framed as a truthful local install path first, such as installing from projects/real-life-workflows, with publish-ready package metadata allowed but no claim that the package is already published.

Grilling decision: npm-installed output should be callable by agent harnesses such as Claude Code, Codex, and opencode. The package must expose skill content or metadata that a harness can import/invoke, not just static README/docs files.

Grilling decision: product direction can include multiple callable skills, but this README/package task should prove one clean callable skill first and describe quarantined imported skill families as candidate future skills only after audit and rewrite.
Grilling decision: the first clean callable packaged skill should be named real-life-workflow-search.
Grilling decision: real-life-workflow-search should return a full workflow-understanding packet by default, not just a short ranked list.
Grilling decision: real-life-workflow-search should write a workflow-understanding packet to disk on every run and report the artifact path back to the operator.

Grilling correction after loading active and quarantined skills plus workflow-foundry-012: packet writes are already governed by capability-dependency artifact rules. The installed/called skill should write packets to the caller or primary project's approved artifact area with provenance, not into node_modules, dependency trackers, dependency AGENTS.md, or dependency skill definitions.
Moved task to PRD after user said to go to PRD. Wrote the README and installable skill-pack PRD, with the next gated action set to issue splitting.
After user requested the full build through completion, split the PRD into five local issue artifacts covering README/counts/hero, npm package surface, callable skill, validator, and review closeout.
Implemented the product README, local npm package metadata, skill manifest, real-life-workflow-search skill, packet writer, count proof, generated hero asset, and README/package validator.
Verified the package surface validator, finder validator, npm pack dry-run, temporary npm install smoke test, and packet writer output. Removed the temporary smoke-test packet after inspection.
Wrote implementation review with no blocking findings and kept task open for workflow-state validation while changed artifacts remain uncommitted.
Committed the implementation checkpoint as acd883e after README/package validation, finder validation, workflow-state validation, npm pack dry-run, install smoke test, and whitespace checks passed.
Marked task done after the implementation commit made the new artifacts no longer require an open task for changed-file validation.
